---
title: "Hardware, Embedded, and Performance-Near Words"
---

# Hardware, Embedded, and Performance-Near Words

Range: `1211-1290`.

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                    | Summary                                                                                                                                           |
| ----- | --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1211  | [accelerator](#rune-1211)               | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1212  | [alignment {HW}](#rune-1212)            | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1213  | [ASIC](#rune-1213)                      | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1214  | [assembly instruction](#rune-1214)      | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1215  | [atomic instruction](#rune-1215)        | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1216  | [bandwidth {HW}](#rune-1216)            | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1217  | [barrier {HW}](#rune-1217)              | A rendezvous word; no party crosses until all required parties arrive.                                                                            |
| 1218  | [bit width](#rune-1218)                 | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1219  | [boot ROM](#rune-1219)                  | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1220  | [branch predictor](#rune-1220)          | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1221  | [bus {HW}](#rune-1221)                  | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1222  | [cache {HW}](#rune-1222)                | A nearness word; it keeps likely things close to reduce future cost.                                                                              |
| 1223  | [cache coherence](#rune-1223)           | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1224  | [cache line {HW}](#rune-1224)           | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1225  | [clock](#rune-1225)                     | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1226  | [clock cycle](#rune-1226)               | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1227  | [co-processor](#rune-1227)              | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1228  | [core {HW}](#rune-1228)                 | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1229  | [CPU](#rune-1229)                       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1230  | [cycle budget](#rune-1230)              | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1231  | [DMA](#rune-1231)                       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1232  | [DRAM](#rune-1232)                      | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1233  | [driver interrupt](#rune-1233)          | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1234  | [EEPROM](#rune-1234)                    | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1235  | [endianness](#rune-1235)                | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1236  | [fence](#rune-1236)                     | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1237  | [firmware {HW}](#rune-1237)             | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1238  | [flash](#rune-1238)                     | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1239  | [FPGA](#rune-1239)                      | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1240  | [frame budget](#rune-1240)              | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1241  | [frequency](#rune-1241)                 | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1242  | [GPIO](#rune-1242)                      | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1243  | [GPU](#rune-1243)                       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1244  | [heat budget](#rune-1244)               | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1245  | [I2C](#rune-1245)                       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1246  | [instruction cache](#rune-1246)         | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1247  | [instruction pointer](#rune-1247)       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1248  | [interrupt service routine](#rune-1248) | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1249  | [ioctl boundary](#rune-1249)            | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1250  | [IPC cost](#rune-1250)                  | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1251  | [ISA](#rune-1251)                       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1252  | [jitter {HW}](#rune-1252)               | A timing-noise word; repeated operations arrive with unstable spacing.                                                                            |
| 1253  | [JTAG](#rune-1253)                      | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1254  | [kernel bypass](#rune-1254)             | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1255  | [latency budget](#rune-1255)            | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1256  | [memory channel](#rune-1256)            | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1257  | [memory-mapped I/O](#rune-1257)         | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1258  | [microcode](#rune-1258)                 | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1259  | [MMIO](#rune-1259)                      | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1260  | [NUMA](#rune-1260)                      | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1261  | [NVRAM](#rune-1261)                     | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1262  | [opcode {HW}](#rune-1262)               | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1263  | [out-of-order execution](#rune-1263)    | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1264  | [PCIe](#rune-1264)                      | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1265  | [pipeline {HW}](#rune-1265)             | A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.                                                                                            |
| 1266  | [power budget](#rune-1266)              | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1267  | [prefetcher](#rune-1267)                | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1268  | [real-time {HW}](#rune-1268)            | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1269  | [register](#rune-1269)                  | A near-metal word; a tiny, privileged vessel close to execution itself.                                                                           |
| 1270  | [register file](#rune-1270)             | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1271  | [reset vector](#rune-1271)              | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1272  | [ROM](#rune-1272)                       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1273  | [RTOS](#rune-1273)                      | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1274  | [runtime budget](#rune-1274)            | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1275  | [sensor loop](#rune-1275)               | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1276  | [SIMD](#rune-1276)                      | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1277  | [SPI](#rune-1277)                       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1278  | [SRAM](#rune-1278)                      | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1279  | [superscalar](#rune-1279)               | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1280  | [thermal throttling](#rune-1280)        | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1281  | [throughput](#rune-1281)                | How much useful work the system can push through a narrow world in bounded time. Shadow: chasing bulk capacity while tail latency burns the user. |
| 1282  | [timing window](#rune-1282)             | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1283  | [TPU](#rune-1283)                       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1284  | [UART](#rune-1284)                      | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1285  | [utilization](#rune-1285)               | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1286  | [vector unit](#rune-1286)               | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1287  | [volatile](#rune-1287)                  | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1288  | [watchdog timer](#rune-1288)            | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1289  | [wire speed](#rune-1289)                | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1290  | [zero-copy {HW}](#rune-1290)            | A nearness-and-efficiency word; data moves without redundant duplication.                                                                         |

## Entries

### [1211] accelerator {#rune-1211}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1212] alignment {HW} {#rune-1212}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1213] ASIC {#rune-1213}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1214] assembly instruction {#rune-1214}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1215] atomic instruction {#rune-1215}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Pocket gloss:** An indivisible machine operation at instruction level.

### [1216] bandwidth {HW} {#rune-1216}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1217] barrier {HW} {#rune-1217}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A rendezvous word; no party crosses until all required parties arrive.

### [1218] bit width {#rune-1218}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1219] boot ROM {#rune-1219}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1220] branch predictor {#rune-1220}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1221] bus {HW} {#rune-1221}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1222] cache {HW} {#rune-1222}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A nearness word; it keeps likely things close to reduce future cost.

### [1223] cache coherence {#rune-1223}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1224] cache line {HW} {#rune-1224}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Pocket gloss:** The coherence-sized chunk of cached memory; too much sharing here hurts.

### [1225] clock {#rune-1225}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1226] clock cycle {#rune-1226}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1227] co-processor {#rune-1227}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1228] core {HW} {#rune-1228}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1229] CPU {#rune-1229}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1230] cycle budget {#rune-1230}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1231] DMA {#rune-1231}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Pocket gloss:** Direct memory access: device moves bytes without making the CPU do every step.

### [1232] DRAM {#rune-1232}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1233] driver interrupt {#rune-1233}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1234] EEPROM {#rune-1234}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1235] endianness {#rune-1235}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Pocket gloss:** The byte order in which multi-byte values are laid out.

### [1236] fence {#rune-1236}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1237] firmware {HW} {#rune-1237}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Pocket gloss:** Software that is closer to the board than the application.

### [1238] flash {#rune-1238}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1239] FPGA {#rune-1239}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1240] frame budget {#rune-1240}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1241] frequency {#rune-1241}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1242] GPIO {#rune-1242}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1243] GPU {#rune-1243}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Pocket gloss:** A massively parallel processor built for throughput-heavy workloads.

### [1244] heat budget {#rune-1244}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1245] I2C {#rune-1245}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1246] instruction cache {#rune-1246}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1247] instruction pointer {#rune-1247}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1248] interrupt service routine {#rune-1248}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1249] ioctl boundary {#rune-1249}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1250] IPC cost {#rune-1250}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1251] ISA {#rune-1251}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Pocket gloss:** Instruction set architecture: the machine contract visible to software.

### [1252] jitter {HW} {#rune-1252}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A timing-noise word; repeated operations arrive with unstable spacing.

### [1253] JTAG {#rune-1253}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1254] kernel bypass {#rune-1254}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1255] latency budget {#rune-1255}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Pocket gloss:** The maximum delay the system is allowed to spend and still remain acceptable.

### [1256] memory channel {#rune-1256}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1257] memory-mapped I/O {#rune-1257}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1258] microcode {#rune-1258}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1259] MMIO {#rune-1259}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1260] NUMA {#rune-1260}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1261] NVRAM {#rune-1261}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1262] opcode {HW} {#rune-1262}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1263] out-of-order execution {#rune-1263}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1264] PCIe {#rune-1264}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1265] pipeline {HW} {#rune-1265}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.

### [1266] power budget {#rune-1266}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1267] prefetcher {#rune-1267}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1268] real-time {HW} {#rune-1268}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1269] register {#rune-1269}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A near-metal word; a tiny, privileged vessel close to execution itself.

**Pocket gloss:** A tiny named storage cell inside the processor.

### [1270] register file {#rune-1270}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1271] reset vector {#rune-1271}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1272] ROM {#rune-1272}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1273] RTOS {#rune-1273}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1274] runtime budget {#rune-1274}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1275] sensor loop {#rune-1275}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1276] SIMD {#rune-1276}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Pocket gloss:** One instruction operating across many data lanes at once.

### [1277] SPI {#rune-1277}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1278] SRAM {#rune-1278}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1279] superscalar {#rune-1279}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1280] thermal throttling {#rune-1280}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1281] throughput {#rune-1281}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** How much useful work the system can push through a narrow world in bounded time.

**Shadow:** chasing bulk capacity while tail latency burns the user.

**Pocket gloss:** How much useful work passes through in bounded time.

### [1282] timing window {#rune-1282}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1283] TPU {#rune-1283}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

### [1284] UART {#rune-1284}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1285] utilization {#rune-1285}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Pocket gloss:** The fraction of available capacity actually being used.

### [1286] vector unit {#rune-1286}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

### [1287] volatile {#rune-1287}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Pocket gloss:** A promise to the compiler that this value may change through unusual means.

### [1288] watchdog timer {#rune-1288}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

### [1289] wire speed {#rune-1289}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

### [1290] zero-copy {HW} {#rune-1290}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A nearness-and-efficiency word; data moves without redundant duplication.

**Pocket gloss:** Near-metal data movement without redundant duplication.
